Joyful.title: The Annunciation of the Angel to Mary
Joyful.meditation: The Angel Gabriel appears to Mary, announcing she is to be the Mother of God.

Joyful.title: The visitation of Mary to Saint Elizabeth
Joyful.meditation: Elizabeth greets Mary: “Blessed art Thou among women and blessed is the fruit of thy womb”

Joyful.title: The nativity of Jesus in Bethlehem
Joyful.meditation: The Virgin Mary gives birth to the Redeemer of the World. “And this will be a sign for you: you will find an infant wrapped in swaddling clothes and lying in a manger.”

Joyful.title: The presentation of Jesus to the Temple
Joyful.meditation: The Blessed Mother presents the Child Jesus in the Temple.

Joyful.title: The finding of Jesus in the Temple
Joyful.meditation: The Blessed Mother finds Jesus in the Temple.

Sorrowful.title: The Agony of Jesus in the garden
Sorrowful.meditation: At Gethsemane, Jesus prays as He contemplates the sins of the world.

Sorrowful.title: The scourging of Jesus at the pillar
The crowning with thorns
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus is mocked and crowned with thorns.

Sorrowful.title: The Carrying of the Cross
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus carries the heavy cross upon His shoulders to Calvary.

Sorrowful.title: The crucifixion and death of Jesus
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus is nailed to the cross and dies after hours of agony.

Glorious.title: The Resurrection of Jesus Christ
Glorious.meditation: Jesus rises glorious and immortal, three days after His death.

Glorious.title: The Ascension of Jesus to Heaven
Glorious.meditation: Jesus ascends into Heaven forty days after His Resurrection.

Glorious.title: The descent of Holy Spirit
Glorious.meditation: The Holy Spirit descends upon Mary and the Apostles.

Glorious.title: The Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary into Heaven
Glorious.meditation: Mary is united with her Divine Son in Heaven.

Glorious.title: The Coronation of the Blessed Virgin Mary, Queen of Heaven and Earth
Glorious.meditation: Mary is gloriously crowned as Queen of Heaven and earth.

Luminous.title: The Baptism of Jesus
Luminous.meditation: The heavens open wide and the voice of the Father declares Jesus the beloved Son.

Luminous.title: The Wedding Feast at Cana
Luminous.meditation: The first of the miracles, Christ changes water into wine thanks to the intervention of Mary.

Luminous.title: The proclamation of the Kingdom of God
Luminous.meditation: Jesus proclaims the coming of the Kingdom of God.

Luminous.title: The Transfiguration
Luminous.meditation: Jesus is revealed in glory to Peter, James and John.

Luminous.title: The Institution of the Eucharist
Luminous.meditation: Christ offers his body and blood as food under the signs of bread and wine.

stations.title: Jesus Is Condemned to Death.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus, after having been scourged and crowned with thorns, was unjustly condemned by Pilate to die on the Cross.
stations.prayer: Jesus, it is because of my sins that You are going to die. Through the merits of Your sorrowful journey, help me in my journey to Heaven. I love You, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love You always and to do Your will.

stations.title: Jesus Takes Up His Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus, in making this journey with the Cross on His shoulders, thought of us, and offered for us, to His Father, the death that He was about to undergo.
stations.prayer: Jesus, I embrace all the suffering that you send to me. Through the merits of your pain in carrying Your Cross, help me to carry my cross with patience and resignation. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Falls the First Time.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus fell for the first time under His Cross. He had been scourged and had a crown of thorns on His head, and the soldiers hit Him. He was in so much pain He could barely walk, but He had to carry the heavy Cross.
stations.prayer: Jesus, the weight of my sins adds to your suffering and makes it infinitely worse. Through the merits of your first fall, deliver me from falling into mortal sin. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Meets His Sorrowful Mother.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus met His Mother Mary along His journey. They loved each other so deeply.
stations.prayer: Jesus, through the sorrow and joy you had in meeting your Mother Mary, help me to be truly devoted to her. Mary, help me to remember in my heart the suffering your Son underwent for me. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Simon Of Cyrene Helps Jesus Carry The Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: When the Jews saw how weak Jesus was, they feared He might die on the way, before He was crucified, so they forced a man named Simon the Cyrenian to carry the Cross behind our Lord.
stations.prayer: Jesus, I accept the cross you give to me, and I accept how you want me to die. I offer all my sufferings and troubles to you. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Veronica Wipes the Face of Jesus.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: A woman named Veronica, seeing Jesus suffering so much, and sweat and blood dripping down His face, gave Him a towel to wipe His face. By a miracle, He left an image of His face on the towel.
stations.prayer: Jesus, your face was once clean and good to look upon, but blood and sweat disfigured it, and Veronica wiped Your face clean. My soul was once clean and beautiful when I was baptized, but sin disfigured it. Through your suffering, wipe my soul clean. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love You always and to do Your will.

stations.title: Jesus Falls a Second Time.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus fell the second time under the Cross. The fall renews all the pain in His body and makes His head throb.
stations.prayer: Jesus, so many times you have forgiven me, and so many times I have sinned again. Through the merits of your second fall, help me and preserve me in your grace until I die. Help me to call on you every time I am tempted. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Meets the Women of Jerusalem.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: These women wept with compassion at seeing Jesus walking to His death. But Jesus said to them, “Weep not for me but for your children.”
stations.prayer: Jesus, I am sorry for all my sins, because of the pain and sadness they cause you, who loves me so much. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Falls the Third Time.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus fell for the third time. He was so weak and the soldiers pushed Him to walk faster, but He could barely move.
stations.prayer: Jesus, by my weakness in temptation, you are going to Calvary. Give me strength to conquer temptation. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Stripped of His Garments.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: The soldiers snatched Jesus' robe off Him.
stations.prayer: Jesus, help me to strip my soul of bad habits so I can give all my love to you, who are so worthy of all my love. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Nailed to the Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Jesus was thrown down onto the Cross. He stretched out His arms and offered His life to the Father for our salvation. The soldiers nailed Him to the Cross and pushed the Cross to stand up.
stations.prayer: Jesus, keep my heart. Keep me always close to you. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love You always and to do Your will.

stations.title: Jesus Dies on the Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: After suffering for three hours on the Cross, Jesus gave up His life to the Father and died.
stations.prayer: Jesus, through my sins, I deserve to be punished, but your death is my hope. Through the merits of your death, give me the grace that when I die, I will die as you want me to. I entrust my soul into your hands. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Taken Down from the Cross.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: Two of Jesus' disciples took Jesus down from the Cross and Mary His Mother held Him close to her heart.
stations.prayer: Mary, sorrowful Mother, pray to your Son for me. Jesus, you have died because you love me. Help me to love you always. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

stations.title: Jesus Is Laid in the Tomb.
stations.verse: We adore you, Christ, and we praise you.
stations.response: Because by your holy Cross, you have redeemed the world.
stations.meditation: The two disciples and Mary laid Jesus in the tomb.
stations.prayer: Jesus, You rose on the third day. Through your Resurrection, make me rise glorious on the last day, to be always with you in Heaven, praising and loving you. I love you, Jesus. I repent of my sins. Help me to never sin again and to love you always and to do your will.

prayers.our_father:
Our Father, who art in heaven,
hallowed be thy name;
thy kingdom come,
thy will be done
on earth as it is in heaven.
Give us this day our daily bread,
and forgive us our trespasses,
as we forgive those who trespass against us;
and lead us not into temptation,
but deliver us from evil.

Amen.
prayers.hail_mary:
Hail, Mary, full of grace,
the Lord is with thee.
Blessed art thou among women
and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus.
Holy Mary, Mother of God,
pray for us sinners,
now and at the hour of our death.

Amen.
prayers.sign_of_cross:
In the name of the Father
and of the Son
and of the Holy Spirit. 

Amen.
prayers.apostles_creed:
I believe in God,
the Father almighty,
Creator of heaven and earth,
and in Jesus Christ, his only Son, our Lord,
who was conceived by the Holy Spirit,
born of the Virgin Mary,
suffered under Pontius Pilate,
was crucified, died and was buried;
he descended into hell;
on the third day he rose again from the dead;
he ascended into heaven,
and is seated at the right hand of God the Father almighty;
from there he will come to judge the living and the dead.

I believe in the Holy Spirit,
the holy catholic Church,
the communion of saints,
the forgiveness of sins,
the resurrection of the body,
and life everlasting.

Amen.
prayers.act_of_contrition:
My God,
I am sorry for my sins with all my heart.
In choosing to do wrong
and failing to do good,
I have sinned against you
whom I should love above all things.
I firmly intend, with your help,
to do penance,
to sin no more,
and to avoid whatever leads me to sin.
Our Savior Jesus Christ
suffered and died for us.
In his name, my God, have mercy.

Amen.
prayers.glory_be: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit; as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.
prayers.hail_holy_queen: Hail, Holy Queen, Mother of Mercy, our life, our sweetness, and our hope. To thee do we cry, poor banished children of Eve; to thee do we send up our sighs, mourning and weeping in this valley of tears. Turn then, most gracious advocate, thine eyes of mercy towards us; and after this our exile, show unto us the blessed fruit of thy womb, Jesus. O clement, O loving, O sweet Virgin Mary.
prayers.o_my_jesus: O my Jesus, forgive us our sins, save us from the fires of hell, and lead all souls to heaven, especially those in most need of thy mercy.

novenas.title: Divine Mercy Novena
novenas.description: Prayed in the nine days before Divine Mercy Sunday

days.intention: All mankind, especially all who have strayed from God
days.prayer: Today, bring to Me all mankind, especially all who have strayed, and immerse them in the ocean of My mercy. In this way You will console Me in the bitter grief into which the loss of souls plunges Me.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon all mankind and especially upon poor souls who have strayed, all encompassed in the Most Compassionate Heart of Jesus. For the sake of His sorrowful Passion, show us Your mercy, that we may praise the omnipotence of Your mercy for ever and ever. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.intention: The souls of priests and religious
days.prayer: Today bring to Me the souls of priests and religious, and immerse them in My unfathomable mercy. It was they who gave Me the strength to endure My bitter Passion. Through them, as through channels, My mercy flows out upon mankind.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the company of chosen ones in Your vineyard — upon the souls of priests and religious; and endow them with the strength of Your blessing. For the love of the Heart of Your Son in which they are enfolded, impart to them Your power and light, that they may be able to guide others in the way of salvation and with one voice sing praise to Your boundless mercy for ages without end. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.prayer: Today bring to Me all devout and faithful souls, and immerse them in the ocean of My mercy. These souls brought Me consolation on the Way of the Cross. They were that drop of consolation in the midst of an ocean of bitterness.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the company of faithful souls, as upon the inheritance of Your Son. For the sake of His sorrowful Passion, grant them Your blessing and surround them with Your constant protection. Thus may they never fail in love or lose the treasure of the holy faith, but rather, with all the hosts of Angels and Saints, may they glorify Your boundless mercy for endless ages. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.intention: Those who do not yet know the mercy of God
days.prayer: Today bring to Me those who do not believe in God and those who do not yet know Me. I was thinking also of them during My bitter Passion, and their future zeal comforted My Heart. Immerse them in the ocean of My mercy.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the souls of those who do not believe in You, and of those who as yet do not know You, but who are enclosed in the Most Compassionate Heart of Jesus. Draw them to the light of the Gospel. These souls do not know what great happiness it is to love You. Grant that they, too, may extol the generosity of Your mercy for endless ages. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.prayer: Today bring to Me the souls of those who have separated themselves from My Church, and immerse them in the ocean of My mercy. During My bitter Passion they tore at My Body and Heart, that is, My Church. As they return to unity with the Church, My wounds heal and in this way they alleviate My Passion.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the souls of those who have separated themselves from Your Son’s Church, who have squandered Your blessings and misused Your graces by obstinately persisting in their errors. Do not look upon their errors, but upon the love of Your own Son and upon His bitter Passion, which He underwent for their sake, since they, too, are enclosed in His Most Compassionate Heart. Grant that they, too, may glorify Your great mercy for endless ages. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.intention: The meek and humble souls, and the souls of little children
days.prayer: Today bring to Me the meek and humble souls and the souls of little children, and immerse them in My mercy. These souls most closely resemble My Heart. They strengthened Me during My bitter agony. I saw them as earthly Angels, who will keep vigil at My altars.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the meek and humble souls, and upon the souls of little children who are enfolded in the abode which is the Most Compassionate Heart of Jesus. These souls bear the closest resemblance to Your Son. Their fragrance rises from the earth and reaches Your very throne. Father of mercy and of all goodness, I beg You by the love You bear these souls and by the delight You take in them: Bless the whole world, that all souls together may sing out the praises of Your mercy for endless ages. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.intention: The souls who especially venerate and glorify the mercy of God
days.prayer: Today bring to Me the souls who especially venerate and glorify My mercy, and immerse them in My mercy. These souls sorrowed most over My Passion and entered most deeply into My Spirit. They are living images of My Compassionate Heart. These souls will shine with a special brightness in the next life. Not one of them will go into the fire. I shall particularly defend each one of them at the hour of passing.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the souls who glorify and venerate Your greatest attribute, that of Your fathomless mercy, and who are enclosed in the Most Compassionate Heart of Jesus. These souls are a living Gospel; their hands are full of deeds of mercy, and their hearts, overflowing with joy, sing a canticle of mercy to You, O Most High! I beg You O God: Show them Your mercy according to the hope and trust they have placed in You. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.prayer: Today bring to Me the souls who are detained in purgatory, and immerse them in the abyss of My mercy. Let the torrents of My Blood cool down their scorching flames. All these souls are greatly loved by Me. They are making retribution to My justice. It is in your power to bring them relief. Draw all the indulgences from the treasury of My Church and offer them on their behalf. Oh, if you only knew the torments they suffer, you would continually offer for them the alms of the spirit and pay off their debt to My justice.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon the souls suffering in purgatory, who are enfolded in the Most Compassionate Heart of Jesus. I beg You, by the sorrowful Passion of Jesus Your Son, and by all the bitterness with which His most sacred Soul was flooded: Manifest Your mercy to the souls who are under Your just scrutiny. Look upon them in no other way but only through the Wounds of Jesus, Your dearly beloved Son; for we firmly believe that there is no limit to Your goodness and compassion. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.prayer: Today bring to Me souls who have become lukewarm, and immerse them in the abyss of My mercy. These souls wound My Heart most painfully. My soul suffered the most dreadful loathing in the Garden of Olives because of lukewarm souls. They were the reason I cried out: ‘Father, take this cup away from Me, if it be Your will.’ For them, the last hope of salvation is to run to My mercy.
days.response: Eternal Father, turn Your merciful gaze upon lukewarm souls who are nonetheless enfolded in the Most Compassionate Heart of Jesus. Father of Mercy, I beg You by the bitter Passion of Your Son and by His three-hour agony on the Cross: Let them, too, glorify the abyss of Your mercy. Amen.
days.closing_prayer:
You expired, Jesus, but the source of life gushed forth for souls, and the ocean of mercy opened up for the whole world. O Fount of Life, unfathomable Divine Mercy, envelop the whole world and empty Yourself out upon us.

O Blood and Water, which gushed forth from the Heart of Jesus as a fount of mercy for us, I trust in You!

Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world. (3 times)

days.title: The Gift of Fear of the Lord
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
The gift of the Fear of the Lord fills us with a sovereign respect for God and makes us dread nothing so much as to offend Him by our actions. It is a gift that fills the soul with reverent awe in the presence of the majesty of God. It is not a servile fear, but a filial fear — the fear of a loving child who dreads to displease a beloved Father.

Come, O blessed Spirit of Holy Fear, penetrate my inmost heart, that I may set You, my Lord and God, before my face forever. Help me to shun all things that can offend You, and make me worthy to appear before the pure eyes of Your Divine Majesty in heaven, where You live and reign in the unity of the ever Blessed Trinity, God, world without end. Amen.

days.title: The Gift of Piety
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
The gift of Piety begets in our hearts a filial affection for God as our most loving Father. It inspires us to love and respect for His sake persons and things consecrated to Him, as well as those who are vested with His authority: His Blessed Mother and the Saints, the Church and its visible Head, our parents and superiors, our country and its rulers.

Come, O blessed Spirit of Piety, possess my heart. Enkindle therein such a love for God that I may find satisfaction only in His service, and for His sake lovingly submit to all legitimate authority. Amen.

days.title: The Gift of Knowledge
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
The gift of Knowledge enables the soul to evaluate created things at their true worth — in their relation to God. It unmasks the pretense of creatures, reveals their emptiness, and points out their only true purpose as instruments in the service of God. It shows us the loving care of God even in adversity, and directs us to glorify Him in every circumstance of life.

Come, O blessed Spirit of Knowledge, and grant that I may perceive the will of the Father. Show me the nothingness of earthly things, that I may realize their vanity and use them only for Thy glory and my own salvation, looking ever beyond them to Thee and Thy eternal rewards. Amen.

days.title: The Gift of Fortitude
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
By the gift of Fortitude the soul is strengthened against natural fear and supported to the end in the performance of duty. Fortitude imparts to the will an impulse and energy which move it to undertake without hesitancy the most arduous tasks, to face dangers, to trample under foot human respect, and to endure without complaint the slow martyrdom of even lifelong tribulation.

Come, O blessed Spirit of Fortitude, uphold my soul in time of trouble and adversity. Sustain my efforts after holiness, strengthen my weakness, give me courage against all the assaults of my enemies, that I may never be overcome and separated from Thee, my God and greatest Good. Amen.

days.title: The Gift of Counsel
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
The gift of Counsel endows the soul with supernatural prudence, enabling it to judge promptly and rightly what must be done, especially in difficult circumstances. Counsel applies the principles furnished by Knowledge and Understanding to the innumerable concrete cases that confront us in the course of our daily duty as servants of God.

Come, O Spirit of Counsel, help and guide me in all my ways, that I may always do Thy holy will. Incline my heart to that which is good; turn it away from all that is contrary to Thy will, and direct me by the straight path of Thy commandments to that goal of eternal life for which I long. Amen.

days.title: The Gift of Understanding
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
Understanding, as a gift of the Holy Spirit, helps us to grasp the meaning of the truths of our holy religion. By faith we know them, but by Understanding we learn to appreciate and relish them. It enables us to penetrate the inner meaning of revealed truths and through them to be quickened to newness of life.

Come, O Spirit of Understanding, and enlighten our minds, that we may know and believe all the mysteries of salvation; and may merit at last to see the eternal light in Thy Light; and in the light of glory to have a clear vision of Thee and the Father and the Son. Amen.

days.title: The Gift of Wisdom
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
Embodying all the other gifts, as charity embraces all the other virtues, Wisdom is the most perfect of the gifts. Of Wisdom it is written: ‘All good things came to me nineteen with her, and innumerable riches through her hands.’ It is the gift of Wisdom that strengthens our faith, fortifies hope, perfects charity, and promotes the practice of virtue in the highest degree.

Come, O Spirit of Wisdom, and reveal to my soul the mysteries of heavenly things, their exceeding greatness, power and beauty. Teach me to love them above and beyond all the passing joys and satisfactions of earth. Help me to attain them and possess them forever. Amen.

days.title: Come, Holy Spirit
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
Come, Holy Spirit, Creator blest, and in our souls take up Thy rest. Come with Thy grace and heavenly aid to fill the hearts which Thou hast made.

O most blessed Light divine, shine within these hearts of Thine, and our inmost being fill! Where Thou art not, we have naught, nothing good in deed or thought, nothing free from taint of ill.

Heal our wounds, our strength renew; on our dryness pour Thy dew; wash the stains of guilt away. Bend the stubborn heart and will; melt the frozen, warm the chill; guide the steps that go astray.

On the faithful, who adore and confess Thee, evermore in Thy sevenfold gift descend. Give them virtue’s sure reward; give them Thy salvation, Lord; give them joys that never end. Amen.

days.title: Mary, Spouse of the Holy Spirit
days.prayer:
Come, O Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of Thy faithful and enkindle in them the fire of Thy love. Send forth Thy Spirit and they shall be created, and Thou shalt renew the face of the earth.

O God, who by the light of the Holy Spirit didst instruct the hearts of the faithful, grant that by the same Holy Spirit, we may be truly wise and ever enjoy His consolations. Through Christ our Lord. Amen.
days.meditation:
O Mary, who by the overshadowing of the Holy Spirit didst become the Mother of the Word Incarnate, we implore thee to obtain for us the gifts of the Holy Spirit. Thou art the temple of the Holy Spirit; through thy intercession may He come to dwell in our hearts. May the Spirit of Truth guard us from all error; may the Spirit of Love inflame our hearts; may the Spirit of Fortitude strengthen us against all our spiritual foes.

O Holy Spirit, Spirit of Truth, come into our hearts; shed the brightness of Thy light on all nations, that they may be one in faith and pleasing to Thee. Amen.

days.title: St. Joseph, Guardian of Jesus
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: St. Joseph was chosen by God above all men to be the earthly guardian of His divine Son. Consider how faithfully Joseph fulfilled this trust — protecting the Child from harm, guiding the Holy Family into Egypt, and returning them safely to Nazareth. In every danger, Joseph was vigilant, obedient, and trusting in God’s providence.

days.title: St. Joseph, Model of Workers
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: Joseph labored as a humble carpenter to provide for the Holy Family. By the work of his hands, he fed, clothed, and sheltered the Son of God and the Blessed Virgin Mary. In his workshop at Nazareth, he sanctified ordinary labor and showed that every honest work, done for the love of God, is a prayer. Ask St. Joseph to bless your daily work and help you offer it to God.

days.title: St. Joseph, Pillar of Families
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: As head of the Holy Family, Joseph created a home where love, faith, and obedience to God flourished. Under his care, Jesus grew in wisdom and grace. Joseph is the model for all fathers and families, showing that a home built on faith and self-sacrifice becomes a sanctuary of peace. Entrust your family to St. Joseph’s intercession today.

days.title: St. Joseph, Protector of the Church
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: Just as Joseph protected Jesus and Mary in their earthly life, so now from heaven he protects the whole Church, the Mystical Body of Christ. Pope Pius IX declared him Patron of the Universal Church. In times of difficulty and crisis, the Church has always turned to Joseph with confidence. Pray for the Church today through his intercession.

days.title: St. Joseph, Man of Silence and Trust
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: Scripture records no words spoken by Joseph, yet his actions speak volumes of faith and trust. When the angel came to him in dreams, he obeyed without question. His silence was not emptiness but fullness — a heart so united to God’s will that words were unnecessary. Ask Joseph to teach you the prayer of silence and the courage of quiet trust in God’s plan.

days.title: St. Joseph, Terror of Demons
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: The Church honors Joseph with the title ‘Terror of Demons’ because of his powerful role as protector of Jesus and Mary. The forces of evil trembled before the man chosen to guard the Redeemer of the world. When you face spiritual struggle or temptation, call upon Joseph. His intercession is a mighty shield against the enemy of our souls.

days.title: St. Joseph, Model of Obedience
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: Four times an angel appeared to Joseph in dreams, and four times he obeyed immediately: taking Mary as his wife, fleeing into Egypt, returning to Israel, and settling in Nazareth. Joseph never argued, delayed, or sought his own understanding first. His prompt obedience teaches us that holiness consists in doing God’s will without hesitation, even when the path is unclear.

days.title: St. Joseph, Patron of a Happy Passing
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: Tradition holds that Joseph passed from this life in the arms of Jesus and Mary — the most blessed departure imaginable. For this reason he is invoked as Patron of a Happy Passing. Entrust your final hour to his care, asking that when the time comes, you too may be surrounded by the love and grace of Jesus and Mary.

days.title: St. Joseph, Hope of the Afflicted
days.prayer:
O St. Joseph, whose protection is so great, so strong, so prompt before the throne of God, I place in you all my interests and desires.

O St. Joseph, assist me by your powerful intercession and obtain for me from your divine Son all spiritual blessings through Jesus Christ, our Lord; so that having engaged here below your heavenly power, I may offer my thanksgiving and homage to the most loving of Fathers.

O St. Joseph, I never weary contemplating you and Jesus asleep in your arms. I dare not approach while He reposes near your heart. Press Him in my name and kiss His fine head for me, and ask Him to return the kiss when I draw my dying breath. Amen.
days.meditation: Joseph knew poverty, exile, danger, and uncertainty, yet he never lost hope in God. He is the patron of all who suffer and all who are in need. Whatever crosses you bear, bring them to Joseph. He who cared for the Son of God will care for you. Place your needs, your anxieties, and your hopes in his faithful hands, and trust that God, through Joseph’s intercession, will provide.

novenas.title: Surrender Novena
novenas.description: A 9-day prayer of total abandonment to God's will
novenas.author: Fr. Dolindo Ruotolo

days.title: Day 1 — Why do you worry?
days.meditation: Why do you confuse yourselves by worrying? Leave the care of your affairs to Me and everything will be peaceful. I say to you in truth that every act of true, blind, complete surrender to Me produces the effect that you desire and resolves all difficult situations.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 2 — Surrender means to close the eyes
days.meditation: Surrender means to close the eyes of the soul, to turn away from thoughts of tribulation and to put yourself in My care, so that only I act, saying "You take care of it." How many things I do when the soul, in so much spiritual and material need, turns to Me, looks at Me and says to Me: "You take care of it," then closes its eyes and rests.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 3 — Suffering is the empire of self-love
days.meditation: You pray for many to be converted and you do not obtain it. You have not yet surrendered truly. Do not worry. I will take care of things. Your prayer should be thus: "Lord, I thank You. You have taken charge of everything." Only when you go through the dark night of suffering will I act.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 4 — Close your eyes and surrender
days.meditation: You often turn to Me and ask Me to take action, but you want Me to adjust your ideas. You are not sick; you want Me to heal you on your terms. Do not be like a sick person who asks the doctor to cure him but dictates to him the remedy. Let Me decide what is best for you.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 5 — You see only the present
days.meditation: You see only the present, but I see the future. The things you worry about will come out well. Do not worry about them. They will bring you blessing and merit. Everything works for your good. Place everything into My hands without reserve. Pray always and pray with confidence.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 6 — I want to act with a free hand
days.meditation: I ask you for an act of complete surrender, and I will do everything. I act when the door is completely open. Surrender to Me completely, and do not want to add anything of your own. I act when I find the nothingness in which to deposit My fullness.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 7 — Do not look at what others do
days.meditation: You are restless; you want to assess everything, direct everything, see to everything. Surrender yourself to grace. Rest in the hands of God and sleep sweetly in the arms of grace. You are too watchful; in My mercy I bring everything to fulfillment.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 8 — Do not worry if I am silent
days.meditation: I never leave you. I never let go of you. My silence means I am working in ways you cannot yet see. The darker the night, the closer the dawn. Trust that I am acting even now. Every trial you offer to Me turns into grace beyond your imagining.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

days.title: Day 9 — How much I love those who surrender
days.meditation: If you only knew how much I love you, you would weep with joy. How dear to My Heart is the soul who surrenders completely! I act in that soul with all My power. I transform sorrow into peace, anxiety into calm, fear into trust. Entrust everything to Me and I will do everything. Let Me act.
days.prayer: O Jesus, I surrender myself to You, take care of everything!

novenas.title: Sacred Heart Novena
novenas.description: Nine days of prayer to the Sacred Heart of Jesus

days.title: Day 1 — The Heart of Jesus, Source of All Consolation
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: Consider the immense love of the Heart of Jesus, which burns with an infinite desire to console all who come to Him. No sorrow is too great, no burden too heavy for the compassionate Heart of Christ.

days.title: Day 2 — The Heart of Jesus, Burning with Love
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The Heart of Jesus is a furnace of love. It was pierced for our sake, and from it flowed blood and water — the fountainhead of the sacraments. Meditate on how deeply God loves you.

days.title: Day 3 — The Heart of Jesus, Loaded Down with Reproaches
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: Jesus endured every insult and reproach out of love for sinners. His Heart bears the weight of every sin, yet it never ceases to love. Offer Him reparation for the ingratitude of mankind.

days.title: Day 4 — The Heart of Jesus, Patient and Full of Mercy
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The patience and mercy of the Sacred Heart are inexhaustible. No matter how often we fall, His Heart remains open to receive us back. Ask for the grace of patience in your own trials.

days.title: Day 5 — The Heart of Jesus, Rich unto All Who Call upon Him
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The Sacred Heart is an inexhaustible treasury of graces for all who invoke it with faith. Call upon the Heart of Jesus with confidence, knowing that He desires to give you more than you can ask.

days.title: Day 6 — The Heart of Jesus, Desire of the Eternal Hills
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The patriarchs and prophets longed for the coming of the Messiah, whose Heart would bring salvation. We who have received this gift should not take it for granted. Renew your gratitude for the gift of faith.

days.title: Day 7 — The Heart of Jesus, King and Center of All Hearts
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The Heart of Jesus is the King of all hearts. He knows every desire, every longing, every sorrow. Enthrone His Sacred Heart in your home and in your life, and let Him reign over all your affections.

days.title: Day 8 — The Heart of Jesus, in Whom the Father Is Well Pleased
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The Father spoke from heaven: "This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased." Through devotion to the Sacred Heart, we unite ourselves to Jesus and become pleasing to the Father. Offer your heart to be transformed by His.

days.title: Day 9 — The Heart of Jesus, Our Peace and Reconciliation
days.prayer:
O most holy Heart of Jesus, fountain of every blessing, I adore You, I love You, and with a lively sorrow for my sins, I offer You this poor heart of mine. Make me humble, patient, pure, and wholly obedient to Your will. Grant, good Jesus, that I may live in You and for You. Protect me in the midst of danger; comfort me in my afflictions; give me health of body, assistance in my temporal needs, Your blessing on all that I do, and the grace of a holy death.

O Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
days.meditation: The Sacred Heart is our peace. In a world of turmoil, anxiety, and division, the Heart of Christ offers reconciliation with God and with one another. Rest in His peace today and carry it to those around you.

novenas.title: Novena to St. Jude
novenas.description: Patron of desperate and hopeless causes

days.title: Day 1
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: St. Jude Thaddeus was one of the twelve Apostles, a relative of Jesus according to the flesh. He preached the Gospel with great passion, and tradition tells us he was martyred for the faith. He is called the patron of desperate cases because his name was similar to that of Judas Iscariot, and so few invoked him — meaning those who did come to him were often at their last resort. Trust that no cause is hopeless with God.

days.title: Day 2
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: St. Jude asks us in his Epistle to "build yourselves up in your most holy faith; pray in the Holy Spirit; keep yourselves in the love of God" (Jude 1:20-21). Even in desperate times, the foundation of our hope is faith, prayer, and love.

days.title: Day 3
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: Recall the words of Jesus: "Ask and it shall be given to you; seek and you shall find; knock and the door shall be opened to you" (Matthew 7:7). St. Jude brings our petitions before the throne of God with the confidence of one who walked with Christ.

days.title: Day 4
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: God's timing is not our timing. St. Jude teaches us patience in prayer. The saints did not receive every answer immediately, but they persevered. Continue to bring your intentions before God through St. Jude's intercession.

days.title: Day 5
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: St. Jude reminds us that nothing is impossible with God. The situations that seem most hopeless to human eyes are precisely where God's power shines most brightly. Surrender your fears to God and trust in His providence.

days.title: Day 6
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: In his Epistle, St. Jude exhorts us to "have mercy on those who doubt" (Jude 1:22). If doubt clouds your prayer, do not despair — ask St. Jude for the grace of stronger faith. He who walked with the Lord understands our struggles.

days.title: Day 7
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: St. Jude was present at the Last Supper and asked Jesus, "Lord, how is it that You will manifest Yourself to us, and not to the world?" (John 14:22). Jesus answered by promising the Holy Spirit. Through St. Jude, ask for a deeper outpouring of the Spirit in your life.

days.title: Day 8
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven as I work with my challenges, particularly (mention your intention here). I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: The tradition of the saints teaches us that answered prayers are often given in ways we did not expect. Trust that God hears your prayer through the intercession of St. Jude, even if the answer comes in a form you did not foresee.

days.title: Day 9
days.prayer:
Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally as the patron of hope. Please intercede on my behalf. Make use of that particular privilege given to you to bring hope, comfort, and help where they are needed most. I praise God with you and all the saints forever. I promise, blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor, to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you. Amen.

St. Jude, pray for us and all who invoke your aid.
days.meditation: As you complete this novena, renew your trust in God's loving providence. St. Jude, who gave his life for the Gospel, intercedes for you before the throne of God. Go forward in hope, knowing that the Lord never abandons those who seek Him.

novenas.title: Miraculous Medal Novena
novenas.description: To Our Lady of the Miraculous Medal

days.title: Day 1 — The Immaculate Conception
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

You know, O Mary, how often our souls have been the sanctuaries of your Son who hates iniquity. Obtain for us, then, a deep hatred of sin and that purity of heart which will attach us to God alone, so that our every thought, word, and deed may tend to His greater glory. Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: Mary appeared to St. Catherine Labouré in 1830, standing on a globe with rays of light streaming from her hands. She said, "These rays symbolize the graces I shed upon those who ask for them." Reflect on the graces you most need and ask Mary to obtain them for you.

days.title: Day 2 — Mary, Full of Grace
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: The greeting of the Angel Gabriel, "Hail, full of grace," tells us that Mary was filled with God's favor from the first moment of her existence. Meditate on the fullness of grace in Mary's life and ask for a share in that grace.

days.title: Day 3 — The Medal of Grace
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: Mary instructed Catherine to have a medal struck in the image she saw. Those who wear it with confidence will receive great graces. The medal is a sign of Mary's constant care for her children.

days.title: Day 4 — Mary, Refuge of Sinners
days.prayer:

Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: Even the greatest sinners find refuge in the Immaculate Heart of Mary. She never turns away anyone who comes to her. Bring to her whatever burdens your conscience and ask for the grace of true repentance.

days.title: Day 5 — Mary, Health of the Sick
days.prayer:

Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: Many miracles of healing have been attributed to the Miraculous Medal. Mary's intercession brings God's healing to body and soul. Pray today for all who are suffering from illness.

days.title: Day 6 — Mary, Comforter of the Afflicted
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: Mary stood at the foot of the Cross and shared in the sufferings of her Son. She understands our sorrow and grief. Turn to her in your afflictions and find comfort in her maternal love.

days.title: Day 7 — Mary, Help of Christians
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: The back of the Miraculous Medal shows the letter M surmounted by a cross, with the hearts of Jesus and Mary below. This reminds us that Mary is always united to her Son in the work of our salvation. Ask her help in living as a faithful disciple of Christ.

days.title: Day 8 — Mary, Queen of All Saints
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

Obtain for us, then, a deep hatred of sin and that purity of heart which will attach us to God alone, so that our every thought, word, and deed may tend to His greater glory. Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: Mary is the Queen of All Saints because she lived the virtues of faith, hope, and charity more perfectly than any other creature. Ask her to lead you along the path of holiness that she walked so faithfully.

days.title: Day 9 — Mary, Our Mother and Advocate
days.prayer:
O Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Our Lord Jesus and our Mother, penetrated with the most lively confidence in your all-powerful and never-failing intercession, manifested so often through the Miraculous Medal, we your loving and trustful children implore you to obtain for us the graces and favors we ask during this novena, if they be beneficial to our immortal souls and the souls for whom we pray.

You know, O Mary, how often our souls have been the sanctuaries of your Son who hates iniquity. Obtain for us, then, a deep hatred of sin and that purity of heart which will attach us to God alone, so that our every thought, word, and deed may tend to His greater glory. Obtain for us also a spirit of prayer and self-denial, that we may recover by penance what we have lost by sin and at length attain to that blessed abode where you are the Queen of angels and of men. Amen.

O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
days.meditation: As this novena concludes, place all your needs and hopes in Mary's hands. She is our Mother and our advocate before the throne of God. Trust in her intercession and live with confidence in God's loving providence.

novenas.title: Christmas Novena
novenas.description: Traditional Advent prayer — repeated 15 times daily
novenas.subtitle: St. Andrew Christmas Prayer · 25 days

days.title: Day 1
days.prayer: Hail and blessed be the hour and moment in which the Son of God was born of the most pure Virgin Mary, at midnight, in Bethlehem, in piercing cold. In that hour, vouchsafe, O my God, to hear my prayer and grant my desires, through the merits of Our Savior Jesus Christ, and of His Blessed Mother. Amen.
days.meditation: This ancient prayer, traditionally attributed to St. Andrew the Apostle, is prayed 15 times each day from November 30 (the Feast of St. Andrew) through Christmas Day. It is one of the oldest Advent devotions, expressing the longing of the faithful for the coming of the Savior.

days.title: Day 2
days.prayer: Hail and blessed be the hour and moment in which the Son of God was born of the most pure Virgin Mary, at midnight, in Bethlehem, in piercing cold. In that hour, vouchsafe, O my God, to hear my prayer and grant my desires, through the merits of Our Savior Jesus Christ, and of His Blessed Mother. Amen.
days.meditation: Repeat this prayer 15 times today, slowly and with devotion. Let each repetition deepen your longing for the coming of Christ — both His coming at Christmas and His coming into your heart.
