novenas.title: Novena to St. Patrick
novenas.description: Patron of Ireland — beloved in New England

days.title: Day 1 — St. Patrick, Apostle of Ireland
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, chosen vessel of grace, apostle of Ireland and father in the faith of a whole nation: obtain for us, we beseech thee, the grace to follow thy example in spreading the Gospel of Christ. May we, like thee, bring the light of faith to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: Patrick was carried away as a slave from Britain to Ireland at the age of sixteen. In his captivity, he turned to God with his whole heart. Later, freed and ordained, he returned to Ireland to bring the faith to those who had enslaved him. Consider how God can transform even our sufferings into instruments of grace.

days.title: Day 2 — The Breastplate of St. Patrick
days.prayer:
Christ be with me, Christ within me,
Christ behind me, Christ before me,
Christ beside me, Christ to win me,
Christ to comfort and restore me.
Christ beneath me, Christ above me,
Christ in quiet, Christ in danger,
Christ in hearts of all that love me,
Christ in mouth of friend and stranger.

O glorious St. Patrick, through your powerful intercession, protect us this day and always. St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: The Lorica, or Breastplate of St. Patrick, is one of the most beloved prayers in Christendom. It expresses total reliance on Christ's protection. Pray it slowly today, placing yourself entirely in Christ's care.

days.title: Day 3 — St. Patrick, Model of Forgiveness
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, you returned with love to the land of your captivity, bearing no hatred toward those who had enslaved you, but only the desire to share with them the treasure of the faith. Obtain for us the grace to forgive those who have wronged us, and to repay evil with good, after the example of our Lord Jesus Christ.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: Patrick could have lived in comfort in Britain or Gaul. Instead, he chose to return to Ireland — to the very people who had enslaved him. His forgiveness was not passive; it was active, generous, and transformative. Ask for the grace to forgive as Patrick forgave.

days.title: Day 4 — St. Patrick, Teacher of the Faith
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, through the symbol of the shamrock you taught the mystery of the Holy Trinity to those who had never heard the Gospel. Grant us the wisdom to explain our faith simply and clearly to those who ask, that we may be ready to give an answer for the hope that is within us.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: Patrick used the humble shamrock to explain the deepest mystery of the faith — one God in three Persons. He met people where they were, using what was familiar to open their hearts to what was eternal. Ask for the gift of sharing your faith with simplicity and joy.

days.title: Day 5 — St. Patrick, Man of Prayer
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, who spent long hours in prayer on the hills and in the wild places of Ireland, teach us to seek God in silence and solitude. Help us to find in prayer the strength to face each day and the wisdom to know God's will.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: In his Confessio, Patrick writes that during his years of slavery, he prayed a hundred times a day and nearly as many at night. Prayer became his life. In our busy lives, ask St. Patrick to help us find time and space for deeper prayer.

days.title: Day 6 — St. Patrick, Courage in Adversity
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, you faced danger and opposition with unwavering courage, trusting always in the providence of God. When we are afraid, when the way is difficult, and when the future is uncertain, obtain for us the fortitude to persevere in faith and hope.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: Patrick faced hostile chieftains, druids, and the constant threat of death. Yet he never turned back. His courage came not from himself but from his absolute trust in God. When you face your own trials, remember that the God who protected Patrick is the same God who watches over you.

days.title: Day 7 — St. Patrick, Patron of New England
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, your spiritual children carried the faith across the ocean to the shores of New England, building churches and communities that endure to this day. Strengthen the faith of all who live in this land. Renew our parishes, bless our families, and keep us faithful to the Gospel.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: The Irish immigrants who came to New England brought with them the faith that Patrick had planted. They built the churches where we worship today. Pray for the renewal of faith in your parish and in your community, through the intercession of St. Patrick.

days.title: Day 8 — St. Patrick, Hope of the Faithful
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, you never despaired, even in the darkest hours of slavery and hardship. Your trust in God's plan was complete. Obtain for us the theological virtue of hope, that we may look beyond our present troubles to the eternal joy that God has prepared for those who love Him.

St. Patrick, pray for us. Amen.
days.meditation: Hope is not optimism; it is the firm expectation that God will fulfill His promises. Patrick's hope was rooted in the cross and resurrection of Jesus Christ. In whatever difficulty you face, ask Patrick to help you anchor your hope in the unfailing love of God.

days.title: Day 9 — St. Patrick, Intercede for Us
days.prayer:
O glorious St. Patrick, as we complete this novena, we place all our needs and intentions in your hands. You who converted a nation, pray for the conversion of our hearts. You who drove out darkness, pray that the light of Christ may shine in our lives and in our world.

St. Patrick, pray for us now and at the hour of our death. Amen.
days.meditation: Today we conclude our nine days of prayer with St. Patrick. Entrust to him all the intentions you have carried throughout this novena. Trust that God, who heard the prayers of Patrick in the fields of Ireland, hears your prayers today.

chaplet.title: The Divine Mercy Chaplet
chaplet.origin: Given by Jesus to St. Faustina Kowalska, 1935

quote.text: Say unceasingly the chaplet that I have taught you.

chaplet.decade_large: Eternal Father, I offer You the Body and Blood, Soul and Divinity of Your dearly beloved Son, Our Lord Jesus Christ, in atonement for our sins and those of the whole world.
chaplet.decade_small: For the sake of His sorrowful Passion, have mercy on us and on the whole world.
chaplet.closing: Holy God, Holy Mighty One, Holy Immortal One, have mercy on us and on the whole world.
chaplet.optional_closing: Eternal God, in whom mercy is endless and the treasury of compassion inexhaustible, look kindly upon us and increase Your mercy in us, that in difficult moments we might not despair nor become despondent, but with great confidence submit ourselves to Your holy will, which is Love and Mercy itself. Amen.
